channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Is Hades 2 Getting A Physical Release on PS5 & Xbox Series X|S? Physical Disc Edition, Explained
item.content:encoded:
The Princess of the Underworld’s bow on PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S was long overdue. While a digital edition was a guarantee, a physical version was not, and there’s a precedent behind Hades 2 and its predecessor, overlooking physical copies.

From the day Hades 2 moved from early access to a 1.0 release, the wait was on for its launch. The Nintendo Switch 2 release came first, but curiously, it was a digital-only release. A physical copy followed, but now we’ve looped back around to the same conversation again with Hades 2 on PS5 and Xbox.

Can You Buy Hades 2 On Disc For PS5 & Xbox Series X|S? Physical Edition, Explained

Right now, there is no physical edition for Hades 2, with neither Supergiant Games nor PlayStation/Xbox advertising a full physical release for the ports.

History has repeated itself once more. Like the Switch 2 version of Hades 2 and Hades 1 prior, the game is getting a full digital release at first. The only way to purchase Hades 2 is through the PlayStation Store and Microsoft Store.

  • More Insider Gaming: Do You Need to Play Hades Before Hades 2?

This means pouring your money into a digital purchase, or maybe you can wait to see what the future holds.

Will Hades 2 Get A Physical Release on PS5 & Xbox Series X|S?

It’s extremely likely (borderline all but confirmed) that Hades 2 will get a full physical edition on PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S, featuring a disc and game box.

Let’s turn back the clock to 2019. At first, Hades was an Epic Game Store exclusive for its early access period, would you believe? It then made its way to Steam, and then Supergiant Games released its sleeper hit indie title to widespread acclaim in 2020.

It came out on Nintendo Switch first in September 2020, but digitally. A full physical release for the Switch happened on March 19, 2021—nearly six months after the digital debut. PlayStation and everyone else finally got a taste of Hades action in September 2021. As you can see, the first game went through a methodical process.

  • More Insider Gaming: Hades 2 Voice Cast Revealed—All Voice Actors Explained

Despite the groundwork being in place, Hades 2 has followed a similar pattern. May 2024 saw Hades 2 go into early access, and it took until September 25, 2025, for a 1.0 launch—exclusively on Nintendo Switch 2.

Fast forward seven months this time for the PS5 and Xbox Series X|S ports. So, if we’re going down the same road, Supergiant Games is presumably allowing the digital release to have its moment in the spotlight, and a full Hades 2 physical release will happen in September or October 2026.

Obviously, it’s not certain, but it’s a likely and logical outcome. So stay tuned with Insider Gaming for more updates on a Hades 2 physical edition. If you want to talk about the game, or gaming in general, why not head on over to the free-to-join Insider Gaming Discord?

Check out Mewgenics overtaking Hades 2 as the most-played roguelike ever for related news.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Crimson Desert Developer Addresses Official Mod Support
item.content:encoded:
Pearl Abyss has stated that they are discussing ideas regarding official mod support for Crimson Desert internally.

The highly anticipated open-world action adventure from Pearl Abyss has finally arrived, and things are looking up for the title after the first patch. The developers are now discussing the way ahead, addressing topics such as DLCs, multiplayer, official mod support, and other aspects. Let’s get into mod support first.

Crimson Desert Devs Are Discussing How to Implement Mod Support

According to a report by South Korean publication INVEN, during a recent shareholder meeting, Pearl Abyss CEO Heo Jin-young was asked whether there were plans to provide a mod kit or to create a modding community, similar to Skyrim’s, to make Crimson Desert a long-term IP. Jin-young responded that the team has been discussing ideas internally regarding official mod support for Crimson Desert; however, there are some challenges.

The CEO stated that “providing mod tools would require disclosing a significant portion of the engine, so there are no concrete plans yet” (Google Translation). At the moment, the team is discussing possibilities while monitoring user gameplay.

With a world like Crimson Desert’s, official mod support will be instrumental in keeping the game relevant for years to come. A case in point is Bethesda’s Skyrim, which released back in 2011 but is still played a lot thanks to countless mods, some of which add story content as well.

In other news, Pearl Abyss has moved the core Crimson Desert development team to their next title, DokeV. Additionally, Crimson Desert has reached another sales milestone, this time selling through 3 million copies. What are your thoughts on Crimson Desert devs addressing official mod support? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Resident Evil Requiem receiving a photo mode through the latest update. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: When Do PS5, PS5 Pro & PlayStation Price Increases Happen In 2026?
item.content:encoded:
Rumours were circulating for a while that Sony was going to increase the prices for their PlayStation hardware, and it appears that the sorry day has finally arrived. On March 27, 2026, the price increases were made official, and they’re set to be implemented sooner than you’d think.

As sad as it might be, it’s not entirely surprising. Netflix has just announced that it’ll be increasing the prices for all subscription tiers, and Xbox Game Pass continues to get more expensive. Still, in a tough economic climate, it’s never easy to see prices hike, so here’s when they’re being bumped, as well as why it’s happening.

PS5, PS5 Pro & PlayStation Portal Price Increase Date, Explained

Every piece of current-gen PlayStation hardware is receiving a price increase worldwide on April 2, 2026.

It’s going to be a dark day for PlayStation fans, especially those who were planning to buy a new console or handheld in the coming months. The price increases are different depending on your region and currency, but generally speaking, they’ve gone up by around 13%.

  • More Insider Gaming: Every PlayStation Studio That’s Closed Since The PS5 Launched

For the average consumer, that’s not a price increase to scoff at. In fact, it’s bound to prevent a sizeable amount of fans from buying a new console, which is ultimately detrimental to the gaming community. However, if the price increases are so universally opposed, why are they happening?

Why Are PS5, PS5 Pro & PlayStation Portal Prices Increasing In 2026?

According to Sony, the price increases are due to ‘continued pressures in the global economic landscape.’

That’s the simple way of putting it, but it’s bound to run far deeper than that. The statement by PlayStation mentions that it’s necessary to ensure they can continue to deliver ‘innovative, high-quality gaming experience to players worldwide.’

  • More Insider Gaming: PlayStation’s ‘Dynamic Pricing’ Offers Wildly Different Discounts For Some Games

How these price increases will directly impact PlayStation remains to be seen. If what they’re saying is true, then it’s been done to account for the various price increases across the market, with various components soaring in price recently, most notably RAM.

Additionally, the cost of AAA development continues to skyrocket, with recent reports suggesting that it can cost over $300 million per title. Considering that Sony is developing a host of AAA first-party titles, the price increases are bound to be excused as a means to support development.

What do you make of the PlayStation price increases? Does it impact your choice to buy a new console? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming PlayStation coverage, check out the exclusive that PlayStation is phasing out the PlayStation Network branding.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/when-do-ps5-ps5-pro-playstation-price-increases-happen-in-2026/

item.title: Crimson Desert Main Development Team Moves to Next Title
item.content:encoded:
Pearl Abyss has announced that the Crimson Desert main development team has moved to their next game, DokeV.

Crimson Desert has been performing really well since its release, with the title recently selling through 3 million copies. The reviews seem to have recovered as well following the game’s first update. Now, the developers are back to the drawing board, working on their next release, which was announced at the same time as Crimson Desert.

Crimson Desert’s Main Team is Now Working on DokeV

According to a report by the South Korean publication INVEN (via GameSpark), the developers of Crimson Desert discussed their upcoming game at a recent shareholder meeting. Pearl Abyss CEO Heo Jin-young explained that they announced three new titles in 2019, and the development process has taken time because they have been working on both the game and the engine simultaneously.

Both games are being developed using the same engine, called Blackspace, with Crimson Desert being the first title to utilize this new technology. The DokeV team was working on “graphic asset production and other tasks in parallel” (Google translation). Still, Crimson Desert required more time than they expected, hence the core development team stayed on the game.

However, now that the game has been released, the main development team for Crimson Desert can focus on DokeV, which will help speed things up. The Ceo also revealed that they estimate “it will take about two to three years from the current timeline to completion and polishing.”

In other news, Pearl Abyss has made Crimson Desert easier in many ways, releasing a massive patch that contains countless fixes. Additionally, Pearl Abyss has taken to social media to apologize for the recent Crimson Desert AI art scandal that rocked the community.

What are your thoughts on Crimson Desert’s main development team moving to work on the next game? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the prices of PS5, PS5 Pro, and PlayStation Portal increasing significantly globally. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Should You Play Crimson Desert On Controller Or Keyboard & Mouse?
item.content:encoded:
Crimson Desert has seen its fair share of complaints since its release, but none of them has been louder than the clunky controls. It’s been a prevalent issue that’s shown up in countless reviews of the game, including Insider Gaming’s own, but there are still two ways to play: with a controller and with a keyboard and mouse.

Each one has its own pros and cons, but when Crimson Desert itself even recommends one over the other, it’s hard to argue that there’s a clear, definitive choice. If you’re still battling with the controls and wondering if you’re using the best option, you’re in luck, as Insider Gaming has it all explained below.

Crimson Desert Keyboard & Mouse Differences Compared To Controller

The obvious difference between using a keyboard and mouse to a controller is that there are way more buttons to press on a keyboard, which can help you navigate the variety of inputs that you’ll need.

A big complaint when playing Crimson Desert is that, because there are so many actions, a lot of them are tied to the same button on a controller. For example, the likes of jumping, interacting, picking up items and mounting your horse are all tied to one button alone!

  • More Insider Gaming: How To Get The Darkbringer Sword In Crimson Desert

There are countless cases where you’ll want to do one thing, but do another instead, but you shouldn’t run into that issue as much on mouse and keyboard, thanks to the vast amount of inputs available. You can also rebind your keys on PC, whilst the option remains unavailable for a controller at the moment.

However, whilst it might sound like using a mouse and keyboard is superior, you might be surprised by which one you’re better off using.

Is Crimson Desert Better On Controller Or Mouse & Keyboard?

Despite the larger number of buttons on a keyboard, it’s still a generally better experience to play Crimson Desert with a controller.

The game even recommends that you use a controller when you boot it up on PC for the first time, and it’s clear that Crimson Desert was developed with a controller in mind, despite the prevalent issues.

  • More Insider Gaming: Crimson Desert Reviews Recover Following First Updates

Sure, you might jump when trying to pick something up, but the general game experience is smoother on a controller, especially during combat. Healing is just down on the d-pad, whilst you’ll be reaching for F3 on a keyboard.

Sometimes, less is more, and unless you’re a veteran flitting between a host of buttons for every unique input, your best bet is probably to pick up a controller and take it easy while exploring the vast world of Pywel.

What are you using to play Crimson Desert? Share your thoughts on the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Crimson Desert content, check out the 1.00.03 patch notes that made the game easier, as well as the Pearl Abyss AI art scandal.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/should-you-play-crimson-desert-on-controller-or-keyboard-mouse/

item.title: Sony Announces Major PS5 & PlayStation Portal Console Price Increases
item.content:encoded:
Rumors started circulating that a significant price increase was coming to the PS5, PS5 Pro, and PlayStation Portal consoles, and the price increase has now been made official.

On April 2, it’s about to become a lot more expensive to buy either of Sony‘s mainline PS5 consoles (the base version and Pro model), and the successful PlayStation Portal.

PlayStation 5, PS5 Pro & PlayStation Portal Price Increases Starting April 2

On March 27, the official PlayStation Blog announced that due to ‘continued pressures in the global economic landscape, we’ve made the decision to increase the prices of PS5, PS5 Pro, and PlayStation Portal remote player globally.’

The changes come into effect from April 2, and there are significant price increases across the board from region to region and on all PS5 and PlayStation Portal hardware.

Check out the full list of price changes below.

U.S.

  • PS5 – $649.99 
  • PS5 Digital Edition – $599.99 
  • PS5 Pro – $899.99

U.K.

  • PS5 – £569.99 
  • PS5 Digital Edition – £519.99 
  • PS5 Pro – £789.99

Europe

  • PS5 – €649.99 
  • PS5 Digital Edition – €599.99 
  • PS5 Pro – €899.99

Japan

  • PS5 – ¥97,980 
  • PS5 Digital Edition – ¥89,980 
  • PS5 Pro – ¥137,980

PlayStation Portal

  • U.S. – $249.99
  • U.K. – £219.99
  • Europe – €249.99
  • Japan – ¥39,980

What do you make of these changes? Is this a worrying sign? Should we expect more price increases in the future? Let us know your thoughts on this through the Insider Gaming Discord.

In case you missed it, check out PlayStation’s ‘Dynamic Pricing’ offering wildly different discounts for some games.

MOBILE APP

Resident Evil Requiem’s Latest Update Adds Photo Mode

Resident Evil Requiem’s latest patch adds a photo mode, enabling players to capture the game’s beauty freely.

Latest Escape from Tarkov Update Makes The Game ‘Easier’

Battlestate Games has released the 1.0.4.0 update in Escape from Tarkov, and in many ways, it makes the game much easier.

Brie Larson Thinks Hollywood Can Learn From Gaming Community

Brie Larson believes it’s time for the film and TV universe to consider what they could learn from the world of video games.

The Eternal Life of Goldman Releasing on Xbox in 2026, Demo Out Now

“Some people get so old, they simply forget to die.” That’s the ominous final line in the trailer for The Eternal Life of Goldman, an upcoming Xbox platformer.

item.title: Is MOUSE: PI For Hire on PlayStation, Xbox, PC, or Nintendo Switch? All Console Platforms & Release, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Not having MOUSE: PI For Hire on different platforms would be a crime, one that a certain Jack Pepper would be an ager beaver (or mouse) to solve. If your purchase is contingent on the game coming to any of PlayStation, Xbox, PcC or Nintendo Switch, read on for full answers.

First, there was Danger Mouse, but now there’s MOUSE: PI For Hire—potentially an even more formidable rodent. It’s gained a lot of traction over the last couple of years, so much so that not even its delay could hinder it. If you’re still excited, check out its platforms.

Can You Play MOUSE: PI For Hire on PS5?

MOUSE: PI For Hire will be squeaking its way along to PlayStation 5 owners and give blue-brand owners a taste of the 1930s.

  • More Insider Gaming: All MOUSE: PI For Hire Editions & Prices

Unless a game is a Nintendo Switch exclusive, 90% of the time, you’re going to be able to play a new game on PS5. It’s no surprise, then, to see MOUSE was one of the first platforms announced.

Can You Play MOUSE: PI For Hire on PS4?

You won’t be able to play MOUSE: PI on PlayStation 4 when the game first launches.

We’ll get into this more later in the guide, but Fumi Games has made it clear that last-gen consoles are getting the short end of the stick—a commonality in 2026. If you want to play MOUSE on PlayStation when it first debuts, you’d better make sure you have a PS5 and it’s set up and ready to go.

Can You Play MOUSE: PI For Hire on Xbox Series X|S?

Current-gen Xbox owners are in luck as MOUSE: PI For Hire is coming to Xbox Series X|S on release day.

That’s right, just like the PS5, the most recent iterations of Xbox are going on a crime-solving hunt as well. There are no cheesy lines here. Just simple good news for Xbox Series X|S owners!

Can You Play MOUSE: PI For Hire on Xbox One?

On the other hand, you, Xbox One users, can only look on in envy, as MOUSE is skipping last-gen Xbox consoles for now.

  • More Insider Gaming: Is MOUSE: PI For Hire On Xbox Game Pass?

The Xbox One suffers the same fate as the PS4. The rubber hose shooter isn’t available for the previous era of Xbox, but all is not lost: A full explanation is on the way.

Can You Play MOUSE: PI For Hire on Nintendo Switch 2?

Nintendo Switch 2 owners can breathe a sigh of relief as the unreal power of the newer Switch 2 means that it can run MOUSE: PI For Hire without an issue.

The Switch 2 has opened up all kinds of possibilities. While the OG Switch was able to run certain big games (at a huge cost), the Switch 2 fares much better.

Can You Play MOUSE: PI For Hire on Nintendo Switch?

The original Nintendo Switch isn’t as lucky, though, and MOUSE: PI For Hire misses out on the Nintendo Switch at release.

Let’s clear the last-gen conversation up, thanks to Fumi Games:

“MOUSE: P.I. For Hire will not see a release on PlayStation 4, Xbox One and Nintendo Switch on April 16, 2026. Details for previous-generation console releases will be revealed at a later date.”

So, the good news is that last-gen ports seem to be happening; we just don’t know when. As always, stay tuned for further updates.

Can You Play MOUSE: PI For Hire on PC?

PC users get MOUSE: PI For Hire on April 16, and there’s no waiting around for a computer port.

  • More Insider Gaming: Does MOUSE: PI For Hire Have A Physical Edition?

So, all in all, modern-day consoles and PC get a tick, whereas last-gen owners have to hold on a bit longer.

We hope that cleared everything up and gave you the answers you sought about MOUSE: PI For Hire’s platforms! If you liked it, or you want more, let us know through the Insider Gaming Discord.

Check out if MOUSE: P.I. For Hire has Early Access or a demo for more content.
